svg.text: How calculate sinus cosinus and tangente for A + B We can use rotations with origin as center and A and B as angles: Rotation ( B ) : Rotation ( A ) cos ( B ) - sin ( B ) cos ( A ) - sin ( A ) sin ( B ) cos ( B ) sin ( A ) cos ( A ) We composite this rotation, we get rotation with A + B as angle : Rotation ( A + B ) : cos ( A + B ) - sin ( A + B ) sin ( A + B ) cos ( A + B ) This matrix is equal to product of matrix for rotations with A and B as angles : cos ( B ) * cos ( A ) - sin ( B ) * sin ( A ) - cos ( B ) * sin ( A ) - sin ( B ) * cos ( A ) cos ( B ) * sin ( A ) + sin ( B ) * cos ( A ) cos ( B ) * cos ( A ) - sin ( B ) * sin ( A ) We have formula for sinus and cosinus : cos ( A + B ) = cos ( A ) * cos ( B ) - sin ( A ) * sin ( B ) sin ( A + B ) = sin ( A ) * cos ( B ) + sin ( B ) * cos ( A ) As tan( A + B ) is equal to sin ( A + B ) / cos ( A + B ) : tan( A + B ) = ( sin ( A ) * cos ( B ) + sin ( B ) * cos ( A ) ) / ( cos ( A ) * cos ( B ) - sin ( A ) * sin ( B ) ) We divise all terms by cos ( A ) * cos ( B ) to retrieve tangente : tan ( A + B ) = ( tan ( A ) + tan ( B ) ) / ( 1 - tan ( A ) * tan ( B ) )
